text: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear energy harvesting model and develop a specialized resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. The design is framed as a non-convex optimization problem aimed at maximizing the total power harvested by energy receivers, while ensuring that multiple information receivers meet their minimum signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements. To solve this, we transform the non-convex objective function from a complex sum-of-ratios form into an equivalent subtractive form, facilitating the derivation of an efficient iterative algorithm. Throughout the iterations, we address rank-constrained semidefinite programs (SDPs) by leveraging SDP relaxation to reach optimal solutions. Our numerical results demonstrate that accounting for non-linear energy harvesting characteristics yields substantial performance gains over designs based on traditional linear models.
